dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8605 IH: Honor Our Commitment Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Correa (for himself, Mr. Tran, and Mr. Johnson of Georgia) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To halt removal of certain nationals of Vietnam, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Honor Our Commitment Act of 2026.

section.header: Limitation on the detention and removal of nationals of Vietnam

subsection.text: Except as provided in subsection (b), an alien may not be detained or removed from the United States on or after the date of enactment of this Act if the alien—

paragraph.text: entered the United States on or before July 12, 1995, and has continuously resided in the United States since such entry; and
is subject to a final order of removal.

subsection.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply to an alien if—

paragraph.text: the Secretary of Homeland Security determines, based on credible facts, that the alien is directly responsible for harming the security of the United States; or
the alien is subject to extradition.

subsection.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security shall authorize an alien described in subsection (a) to engage in employment in the United States and provide such alien with an employment authorized endorsement or other appropriate work permit.

section.header: Notice for certain Vietnamese nationals with removal orders

subsection.text: Not later than 60 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Homeland Security shall provide notice of the provisions of this Act to each alien described in section 2(a).

The notice described in subsection (a) shall include information explaining the requirements and instructions for filing a motion to reopen removal proceedings under section 240(c)(7) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1229a(c)(7)).

Notwithstanding any other provision of law, an individual or entity who has been harmed by a violation of this Act may file an action in an appropriate district court of the United States to seek declaratory or injunctive relief.

Nothing in this Act may be construed to preclude an action filed pursuant to subsection (a) from proceeding as a class action (as such term is defined in section 1711 of title 28, United States Code).
